paper_title: Peatland Development in Greater Pamba Basin

methods.method_name: Field Data Collection and Borehole Sampling
methods.description: Systematic fieldwork was carried out in river basins for data collection on landform features and borehole sites for subsurface sample collection. Borehole core sediments were collected using a rotary drilling unit with a Split Sediment Sampler.

steps.description: Systematic fieldwork was carried out in the Achankovil, Pamba, and Manimala river basins for the collection of data on various landform features and also for locating borehole sites for subsurface sample collection.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct fieldwork to collect data on landform features and locate borehole sites

steps.description: The borehole core sediments were collected using rotary drilling unit fitted with Split Sediment Sampler.
steps.searchable_summary: collect borehole core sediments using rotary drilling and Split Sediment Sampler

steps.description: After documenting the gross lithological details, the borehole cores are sectioned at 10 cm intervals and sub-samples from selected depths were packed in neatly labeled Polyethylene bags for further laboratory analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: section borehole cores and prepare sub-samples for analysis

methods.description: The sediment samples were subjected to textural analysis and organic carbon content determination. Palynological examinations were conducted on selected samples.

steps.description: The sediment samples were subjected to textural analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: perform textural analysis on sediment samples

steps.description: The organic carbon (C-org) contents of the sediments were determined following the wet oxidation method of El Wakeel and Riley.
steps.searchable_summary: determine organic carbon content using wet oxidation method

steps.description: Selected samples of the organic matter rich sediments were subjected to palynological examinations.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct palynological examinations on organic-rich sediment samples

methods.description: The nitrogen and carbon stable isotope composition of selected core samples was determined using mass spectrometry after specific preparation steps.

steps.description: The nitrogen isotope composition (δ15N) was determined with a Thermo Finnigan Delta Plus gas isotope ratio mass spectrometer after high temperature combustion in a Flash 1112 EA elemental analyzer.
steps.searchable_summary: determine nitrogen isotope composition using mass spectrometry

steps.description: The carbon isotope composition (δ13Corg) was determined similarly after removal of carbonates by adding 1N HCl and subsequent drying at 40°C.
steps.searchable_summary: determine carbon isotope composition after carbonate removal
